---
title: "Animation as a Tool"
---

Explore how families and media creator can use animation as a tool for children's learning and development.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### Do Children Know "Villains" Are Bad?

:::::: columns
::: {.column width="30%"}
![](images/villains_thumb.png)
:::

::: {.column width="5%"}
:::

::: {.column width="65%"}
No character is entirely good or entirely bad, and children notice those shades of gray. Animated stories give families a safe space to explore empathy, helping children understand that even the "villains" have complex feelings and people who love them.

[Full post coming soon →](posts/villains-moral-growth.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary style="padding: 1px 6px; font-size: 0.75rem; vertical-align: middle;"}
:::
::::::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### How Animation Can Be a Tool for Tough Topics

:::::: columns
::: {.column width="30%"}
![](images/death_thumb.png)
:::

::: {.column width="5%"}
:::

::: {.column width="65%"}
Talking about death doesn't have to be scary. Animated films and storytelling provide a starting point for children to ask big questions about life and biology.

[Full post coming soon →](posts/tough-topics.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary style="padding: 1px 6px; font-size: 0.75rem; vertical-align: middle;"}
:::
::::::

------------------------------------------------------------------------
